title: paul_new_perspective_deep_research
original_request: Deep research study on the New Perspective on Paul (NPP): examining the scholarly debate initiated by E.P. Sanders, James D.G. Dunn, and N.T. Wright regarding Paul's theology of justification, 'works of the law,' the relationship between faith and Torah observance, and Second Temple Judaism. Analyze key Pauline passages (Galatians 2:15-21, Romans 3:20-28, Romans 4, Philippians 3:2-11, Ephesians 2:8-10), Greek terminology (erga nomou, dikaiosyne theou, pistis Christou), and provide a balanced, theologically faithful assessment comparing traditional Reformation readings with the New Perspective.
